title: Did Israel surprise US with strikes on Iran oil depots? White House reportedly sent 'WTF' message
content: The White House expressed surprise and disapproval after Israel struck Iranian oil facilities, a move not anticipated by the Trump administration. Officials feared the attacks could rally Iranians, boost oil prices, and negatively impact global energy markets. While Israel stated the depots supplied the regime, Washington conveyed strong disagreement, with concerns about rising gas prices.

title: Bahrain's Bapco declares 'force majeure' after Iran strikes: What it means
content: Bahrain's state oil company declared force majeure on shipments after Iranian strikes ignited a fire at its largest oil facility. The attack, which caused material damage but no casualties, escalates regional tensions and disrupts vital energy corridors. This development follows a series of strikes on energy installations amid the widening conflict.

title: ‘Stop celebrating milestones’: Gambhir’s strong message after India’s T20 WC triumph
content: India's head coach Gautam Gambhir emphasized that trophies, not individual milestones, are paramount for team success. He highlighted this philosophy after India's T20 World Cup victory, urging a shift in focus from personal records to collective achievements. Gambhir praised Suryakumar Yadav and Sanju Samson for embracing this team-first approach, prioritizing wins over statistics.

title: Sam Altman responds to Jensen Huang’s ‘like mad’ remark on OpenAI demand
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang revealed significant investments to boost OpenAI's computing power across multiple cloud platforms, including AWS. This move, described as backing a 'consequential company,' highlights Nvidia's strategic shift towards supporting AI inference. The tech giant is also reportedly developing a new processor specifically for OpenAI's needs, marking a major evolution in its business strategy.

title: Nifty50 trades below 24,000, Sensex tumbles 2,000 points — reasons why stock market crashed
content: Indian stock markets, Nifty and Sensex, opened the week lower amid escalating Middle East conflict, following a significant market cap erosion last week. Analysts anticipate geopolitical developments, particularly their impact on global crude oil prices, will dictate market direction. Investors are also closely monitoring foreign investor flows and global cues for sentiment shifts.

title: Oil above $100 as Iran war disrupts supply: Will G7 tap emergency reserves?
content: G-7 nations are considering a joint release of strategic oil reserves to stabilize surging crude prices amid the ongoing Middle East war. Finance ministers will discuss the coordinated drawdown, which would be in conjunction with the International Energy Agency. This move aims to alleviate supply disruptions impacting global consumers and industries.

title: Kanpur man kills minor sister-in-law after marrying her in wife’s absence
content: A man in Etawah district allegedly married his minor sister-in-law before brutally murdering her with a brick. He then surrendered to the police. The incident followed the girl's disappearance and the accused posting a wedding photo on social media, prompting the family to seek their return.

title: Groups representing 700,000 Amazon, Google, Microsoft workers urge firms to ‘do right’
content: Employees from major tech firms like Amazon, Google, and Microsoft are demanding their companies reject Pentagon contracts that compromise AI safety guardrails.  These workers are concerned about the potential for mass surveillance and AI-powered weaponry without human oversight, urging transparency and ethical practices.

title: CBSE Class 12 maths exam analysis: Students call paper moderately difficult
content: The CBSE Class 12 Mathematics Board Exam 2026 was conducted on March 9 from 10:30 am to 1:30 pm, with students describing the paper as moderately difficult overall. Many flagged Section D as the toughest. Students Tanishka and Komal said the long-answer questions were lengthy. The exam carried 80 theory marks and 20 internal assessment marks, with evaluation through the On-Screen Marking system.

title: From London luxury homes to shell firms: The wealth network linked to Iran’s new supreme leader
content: Mojtaba Khamenei is set to become Iran's Supreme Leader following his father's death. Reports indicate he controls billions in assets, including luxury properties and a vast investment empire, often held through shell companies and intermediaries. His potential succession raises concerns about hereditary leadership and could escalate geopolitical tensions.

title: Israel accused of using white phosphorus in Lebanon: Why the weapon is controversial
content: Human Rights Watch alleges Israel unlawfully used white phosphorus shells in a Lebanese village, sparking concerns over civilian harm. The controversial incendiary munition ignites on contact with oxygen, causing severe burns and fires. Meanwhile, Hezbollah reports engaging Israeli helicopter-borne forces in eastern Lebanon, escalating regional tensions amid ongoing Israeli strikes across the country.

title: Sundar Pichai congratulates Team India on winning the ICC Men's T20 World Cup
content: India achieved a historic T20 World Cup victory, becoming the first nation to defend its title and win on home soil. Their dominant performance against New Zealand, highlighted by exceptional batting and bowling, secured their third T20 World Cup title. Tech leaders Sundar Pichai and Satya Nadella congratulated the team on their remarkable achievement.

title: ‘Contact with Iran leadership difficult’: Jaishankar spells out India’s position on Middle East war
content: India advocates for dialogue and diplomacy to ease Middle East tensions, with PM Modi closely monitoring the situation. External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar highlighted concerns for over a crore Indians residing in the Gulf and the region's importance for energy security. The conflict's escalation has led to significant disruption and the tragic loss of two Indian mariners.

title: ‘Samson is a combination of Kohli & Rohit’: Kumble after T20 WC masterclass
content: Sanju Samson's stellar T20 World Cup performance, especially in the knockouts, has drawn high praise. Legendary spinner Anil Kumble sees Samson as a unique blend of Virat Kohli's calculated approach and Rohit Sharma's aggressive power-hitting.  Samson's remarkable run, including record-breaking scores, propelled India to victory, showcasing his adaptability and explosive talent.

title: DTC bus mows down 2 in Delhi's Nangloi, set on fire by angry locals
content: Tragedy struck Outer Delhi's Nangloi Monday morning when a DTC bus fatally struck an e-rickshaw and several other vehicles. The devastating collision sparked outrage, leading locals to set the bus ablaze. Authorities quickly responded, bringing the situation under control and launching an investigation into the harrowing incident.

title: Video shows thick black smoke rising after Iran hits Bahrain's largest oil refinery — watch
content: Bahrain's largest oil refinery, Bapco, was hit by an aggressive strike, allegedly by Iran's new hardline leadership. Thick black smoke billowed from the facility in Sitra following reports of injuries and damage. This attack occurred amid escalating regional tensions and follows an earlier Iranian drone and ballistic missile strike on the critical energy infrastructure.

title: Why same car has different prices across Indian cities: Explained
content: Curious why car prices differ across Indian cities? It's not just the manufacturer's price. State road taxes, RTO registration fees, and insurance costs significantly impact the final on-road price.  For instance, a Hyundai Creta's price can vary by over Rs 1 lakh between cities like Delhi and Hyderabad due to these state-specific charges and fuel type taxes.

title: Why South India’s real estate market is set to surge on massive infrastructure investments
content: India's massive infrastructure spending, particularly in South India, is poised to ignite real estate growth.  With billions invested in high-speed rail, roads, and new cities, expect significant appreciation in land and property values.  This presents a golden opportunity for investors and developers to capitalize on burgeoning economic activity and job creation across the region.

title: 'Intensity with a smile is a killer combo': Dhoni playfully praises Gambhir
content: India has won its third T20 World Cup title. They defeated New Zealand in the final match. This victory marks a historic achievement for the Indian cricket team. MS Dhoni congratulated the team and coach Gautam Gambhir. Jasprit Bumrah was named Player of the Match for his outstanding bowling performance.

title: Liquor policy case: Delhi high court defers ED case against Kejriwal, Sisodia
content: The Delhi High Court has halted departmental action against a CBI officer investigating the excise policy case. The court also paused proceedings in the ED case, pending a decision on the CBI's appeal against the discharge of Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia, and others.

title: Woman at centre of Uttam Nagar clash held as crackdown intensifies
content: Eleven individuals, including three women, are detained in connection with the Holi night murder of Tarun Khatik in Uttam Nagar. The clash reportedly erupted from a Holi celebration dispute between neighboring families.  Authorities have invoked the SC/ST Act, and a demolition drive targeting illegal structures linked to an accused was conducted amidst heavy security.

title: Shakun Group MD Vallabh Maheshwari killed after BMW overturns in Rajasthan’s Alwar
content: A tragic accident on a Rajasthan expressway claimed the life of Vallabh Maheshwari, 62, managing director of Shakun Group. His BMW overturned after colliding with a median and culvert wall near Alwar. Maheshwari was en route from Jaipur to Mathura when the driver lost control. He was declared dead at an Alwar hospital after initial treatment.

title: Crude oil surpasses $100: WTI up 30%, brent crude reaches $118; what it means?
content: Middle East conflict intensifies, driving oil markets past $100 a barrel as Brent crude reached $118. Fears of energy supply disruption and blocked shipping routes, particularly through the Strait of Hormuz, have caused significant price surges. This volatility impacts global energy supplies and the cost of everyday goods.

title: Dubai airports resume limited flights; passengers advised to confirm timings
content: Dubai International Airport and Al Maktoum International Airport are resuming operations with select airlines. Major UAE carriers are restarting services on some routes. Passengers must confirm flights with their airline before traveling. Schedules remain subject to change due to ongoing regional tensions. Travelers are urged to stay updated on airline announcements for safety and smooth travel.

title: T20 WC: The Rohit Sharma playbook Suryakumar Yadav keeps in his pocket
content: India's T20 dominance stems from a fearless, all-attack mindset, initiated by Rohit Sharma and refined by Suryakumar Yadav. This philosophy, prioritising aggressive batting from the outset and leveraging all-rounders, has transformed the team into a T20 powerhouse, even after key retirements.  The approach, despite occasional pressure, has consistently delivered results.

title: Dhurandhar: The Revenge advance booking crosses Rs 18 crore
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is making waves with record-breaking advance box office collections. The film has already garnered significant pre-sale figures in North America, eyeing USD 2 million, and domestic bookings have surpassed Rs 18 crore. This strong start suggests a massive post-pandemic release for the highly anticipated sequel.

title: Odisha school principal thrashed, garlanded with shoes over ‘affair’ claims
content: The president of a school managing committee and three others were arrested for assaulting an in-charge headmaster in Sundargarh district. The headmaster was allegedly garlanded with shoes and paraded through the village on suspicions of an illicit relationship with the SMC president's wife. Police are searching for additional accused.

title: Ashutosh Maharaj, who filed sex abuse case against seer in UP, 'attacked' on train
content: Ashutosh Maharaj, who filed an FIR against Swami Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati for alleged sexual exploitation, reported being attacked by an unidentified person on the Rewa Express. The incident occurred in the toilet of his AC first-class coach as the train neared Sirathu station.

title: Khamenei dead, son Mojtaba named Iran’s supreme leader: Where things stand on day 10 of war
content: A devastating 10-day conflict has erupted between the US, Israel, and Iran, claiming over 1,600 lives and displacing hundreds of thousands in Lebanon. Key events include the assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader, retaliatory strikes, Hezbollah opening a northern front, and escalating regional tensions threatening global energy markets. The war has drawn in Gulf states and disrupted vital shipping routes.

title: Rupee hits all-time low to reach 92.5 against US dollar as oil prices surpass $100
content: The Indian Rupee faced significant pressure, nearing a record low due to soaring crude oil prices and a stronger US dollar, exacerbated by Middle East tensions.  Foreign investor outflows and a sharp decline in domestic equities further impacted the currency. Experts warn of further weakening if oil prices remain elevated, potentially pushing the rupee to 93.00.

title: When will the Iran war end? Trump hints at 'mutual' decision with Netanyahu
content: US President Trump stated that the decision to end the war with Iran will be mutually decided with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu. Trump claimed intervention prevented Iran from destroying Israel, and he will make the final decision on the conflict's duration. The White House expects the war to last four to six weeks.

title: Firm ideas, flexible tactics: How Gambhir helped India tame T20’s fickle nature
content: Head coach Gautam Gambhir's T20 World Cup triumph solidifies his dominance in the format, leveraging IPL experience and conviction.  His 'form over reputation' mantra, despite controversial selections like dropping Gill and Pant, has reshaped the Indian T20 team.  While successful in T20s, his adaptability for longer formats remains a key question.

title: High risk, high reward: How ‘Total T20’ fuelled India’s march to World Cup glory
content: India's cricket team is forging a new T20 dynasty, mirroring Rinus Michels' 'Total Football' with a 'Total T20' approach. Since their 2024 World Cup win, they've dominated bilateral series with aggressive batting, multi-dimensional players, and a focus on impact over traditional roles. This strategy, led by Suryakumar Yadav and Gautam Gambhir, prioritises T20 success and has created an intimidating aura.

title: Spiritual school in Pune shut over alleged abuse, 47 taken to child-care homes
content: A spiritual education center in Alandi has been closed amidst serious allegations of misconduct involving minor girls and breaches of child protection laws. Authorities have moved 47 children, including four girls, to government shelters. Investigations revealed the center operated without proper permissions and did not meet essential hygiene and safety standards, leading to an urgent shutdown and a police probe.

title: Bride jilts groom & walks off stage with boyfriend in Andhra Pradesh
content: A dramatic turn of events at an Andhra Pradesh wedding saw a bride abandon her groom mid-ceremony, declaring her love for another man. The shocking revelation brought the festivities to a standstill, leaving over 500 guests stunned. Police intervened to prevent a clash, and elders are now mediating a resolution between the families.

title: 'Trust and faith': Gautam Gambhir reveals India’s success mantra
content: Gautam Gambhir, fresh off coaching India to a T20 World Cup victory, emphasized his accountability lies with the team, not social media. He highlighted a fearless, high-risk, high-reward strategy and a selection philosophy built on trust, not hope. This approach saw him back players like Sanju Samson and Abhishek Sharma, underscoring his commitment to unwavering belief in his squad.

title: Video shows Iranian cluster bomb submunitions raining over Israel; why the weapon is tricky
content: Iran launched multiple ballistic missiles at Israel, including at least one with a cluster bomb warhead, causing widespread damage and injuring six people. The use of cluster munitions, which scatter numerous submunitions over a large area, complicates missile defense systems and poses significant risks to civilians due to unexploded bomblets.

title: Pillars of the T20 dynasty: Key inflection points for India from 2024–26
content: India's T20 evolution post-2024 World Cup win saw a shift to a younger, aggressive style. Key changes included moving on from Hardik Pandya as captain, appointing Suryakumar Yadav as T20-only skipper, and prioritising intent over averages with selections like Shubman Gill's exclusion. Backing Sanju Samson and maintaining left-right batting combinations further fueled this relentless winning machine.

title: Bengaluru night crash horror: Dragged under MUV, scooter erupts in flames on busy road
content: A speeding MUV collided with a gearless scooter on Jayamahal Main Road, dragging it over 100 meters and causing it to catch fire. The scooter rider sustained minor injuries and was hospitalized. Police are searching for the MUV driver who fled the scene after the incident.

title: Women's Bracketology: Championship Sunday brings clarity to No. 1 seeds, top 16
content: Texas locked up the No. 3 overall seed, Duke moved to a No. 2 and West Virginia should host NCAA tournament games.

title: Sources: Rams reach record deal with McDuffie
content: The Los Angeles Rams and All-Pro cornerback Trent McDuffie reached agreement on a record four-year, $124 million extension that includes $100 million guaranteed, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Sunday night.

title: Raiders trading for Bills CB Johnson, source says
content: The Raiders are sending a sixth-round pick to the Bills in exchange for veteran cornerback Taron Johnson and a seventh-round selection, a source told ESPN.

title: Texas rolls SC for 1st women's SEC tourney title
content: Madison Booker scored 18 points as No. 4 Texas sprinted to a 14-0 lead against No. 3 South Carolina en route to the Longhorns' first women's SEC tournament title.

title: Spartans' Fears gets tech for another groin kick
content: Michigan State point guard Jeremy Fears Jr. received another technical foul for a kick toward a player's groin area.

title: Iraq coach asks FIFA to reschedule its WC playoff
content: Graham Arnold is asking FIFA to come to Iraq's aid as the conflict between neighboring Iran and the United States threatens to derail his plans to help his team to its first World Cup in 40 years.

title: FB Ingold joins Chargers, reunites with McDaniel
content: The Chargers agreed to terms on a contract with fullback Alec Ingold, reuniting him with offensive coordinator Mike McDaniel.

title: WVU avenges losses to TCU, wins Big 12 tourney
content: No. 15 West Virginia avenged two regular-season losses to No. 10 TCU by beating the reigning Big 12 tournament champion 62-53 in the conference title game Sunday.

title: Queens earns NCAA berth in first year eligible
content: Chris Ashby made 10 3-pointers and scored 34 points to lead Queens University to an NCAA berth in its first year of eligibility with a 98-93 win over Central Arkansas in the Atlantic Sun tournament.

title: 🔮 Men's Bubble Watch: Iowa's positive outlook
content: Entering the Big Ten tournament, the Hawkeyes remain in good position despite six losses in eight games.

title: Last-minute NFL free agency nuggets: What we're hearing on Eagles plans, top RBs and WRs
content: What are we hearing as free agency nears? We give you the latest buzz on top players and aggressive teams.

title: Who aced the trade deadline? Six winners, six losers
content: The final week was a fun one, with some late shockers. Here's who feels great now -- and who might not.

title: 'LOL THATS IT?': MMA fighters react to UFC White House card
content: The polarizing fight card generated online comments from stars such as Conor McGregor and Sean O'Malley.

title: Predicting the USWNT's starting lineup for next year's World Cup
content: With the 2027 Women's World Cup closer than you think, we look at the USWNT's roster and forecast the 11 players Emma Hayes will count on for the tournament.

title: The pride and power of Iowa State basketball's Audi Crooks
content: Audi Crooks hears the haters. But Iowa State's star inherited resilience from her mom and joy from her dad. Her high-scoring game is the ultimate counterpoint.

title: F1's new rules create 'Mario Kart' racing in Australia season opener
content: F1 preseason has been dominated by criticism of its new rules, and while Australia's season opener boasted lots of passing, the critiques didn't stop.

title: Reid's updated NFL draft QB Hot Board: Strengths and weaknesses for the top 12 passers
content: Who are the best quarterbacks in the 2026 class? We sized up the top 12, starting with Fernando Mendoza and Ty Simpson.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Bernardo Silva set to leave Man City in summer
content: Bernardo Silva is expected to leave Man City, while Real Madrid have taken an interest in Newcastle's Sandro Tonali. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: I grew up in a family where women were bosses: Rahul Gandhi
content: "the boss of my family was my grandmother and I have always in my family, like on this table, been outnumbered by women" women are generally more intelligent than men, he says . "when women recognise their potential, they can bring about extraordinary change," he adds .

title: 'President standing, you're sitting': Photo in hand, Mamata flags ‘proof of who shows respect’
content: KOLKATA CM Mamata Banerjee dug out an undated picture of PM Modi seated and president Droupadi Murmu standing at an event . she flagged it as “proof of who actually shows respect” while contesting his charge that govt had “gravely insulted” the presidential chair with a protocol breach at Siliguri .

title: Namibian cheetah gives birth to five cubs in Kuno National Park; total count rises to 53
content: In a post on X, Environmental Minister Bhupender Yadav said this marks an important milestone in India’s cheetah conservation journey

content: Maharashtra Minister Chandrasekhar Bawankule will be Central observer for the Rajya Sabha elections in Odisha,” a notification said

title: SIR in W.B.: Supreme Court to consider plea against deletion of electors from electoral rolls
content: “These are electors. They had voted earlier and now their documents have not been taken,” Senior lawyer Maneka Guruswamy said

content: “During the probe, the CBI has gathered material that needs further questioning and clarification from the actor,” a CBI officials said

title: Meghalaya CM Sangma condemns Delhi assault, says racial bullying against northeast people not acceptable
content: The physical attack on two of our people from Manipur and Assam near the Saket Court in Delhi is sickening, Mr. Sangma said

title: Kerala private sector nurses’ strike disrupts hospital services; protests in Kozhikode
content: Though Kerala government recently revised pay structure of nurses working in private hospitals, United Nurses Association claims that certain hospitals are not willing to implement it

content: Inaugurating the 250-year-old lake, CM Naidu directed officials to take steps towards lake policing and ensure that women who visit the waterbodies do not face issues

title: BJP urges Election Commission to hold West Bengal Assembly election in not more than 3 phases
content: A key concern raised by the BJP delegation related to the deployment and utilisation of nearly 400 companies of central forces currently stationed in the State

title: EV firm Ultraviolette launches battery aimed at sustainable mobility
content: Customers can now own an Ultraviolette X-47 starting at ₹1,49,000, while subscribing to the battery beginning at ₹2499 per month,” said the company.

title: Kerala govt to honour T20 World Cup hero Sanju Samson with civic reception
content: General Education Minister V. Sivankutty says government has communicated the decision to Sanju Samson and is awaiting the cricketer’s nod

title: Class 10 student dies as thatched hut catches fire near Minjur
content: Early Monday morning, the thatched hut suddenly caught fire and the flames spread rapidly. Srinivasan, who was sleeping inside the house, got trapped in the fire

title: Watch: From protests to power: RSP’s victory reshapes Nepal politics | The Hindu Editorial
content: Nepal’s March 5 election has delivered a dramatic political upheaval, with the four-year-old Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) securing a commanding mandate. Led by Balendra Shah, the party defeated veteran leaders including K.P. Sharma Oli in a vote driven by youth anger over corruption and instability.

title: A weekend in Jodhpur: Inside Chandelao Garh and the city’s legendary street food trail
content: Stay at an ancestral home-turned-boutique hotel, explore community craft initiatives, and eat your way through jalebis, gulab jamun sabzis and rabri ladoos

content: The full bench of the Election Commission of India (ECI) was at Kalighat to offer their prayers at the Kali temple, which is also close to the residence of Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee

title: Focus on subjects like AI, automation needs to be increased: PM Modi
content: We will have to accelerate the process of linking our education system to the real world economy even further, he said

title: U.S. urged India to buy Russian oil already at sea to ease supply fears: Energy Secretary Chris Wright
content: The move, Energy Secretary Chris Wright said is a short-term, pragmatic effort to stabilise the market and did not signal any change in Washington's policy towards Russia

content: “They were trying to take shelter in the border area of Bongaon with the intention of crossing back into Bangladesh when the opportunity arises,” the statement said

content: FIR says the trawling boat sank following the accident, causing a loss of about ₹1.5 crore to its owner

title: Schools reopen in Kashmir
content: Schools, colleges and universities were closed for a week from last Monday after protests erupted across the Valley following Khamenei’s killing

title: Swachh Poes: how a gated community in Chennai is promoting the goals of Clean India Mission
content: A resident of Aashiana Apartments at Alwarpet in Chennai, Anita Hegde narrates how volunteer-residents from the gated community are nudging their neighbours on Poes Road and Rajakrishna Road to practise source segregation of waste, keep their neighourhood spic and span and also adopt other sustainability-promoting practices

title: Around 30 students fall ill with diarrhoea at AIIMS-Mangalagiri, 10 under observation
content: According to information from the management, the symptoms began on Thursday (March 6) and Friday (March 7) among undergraduate students.

content: The controversial tenure of Governor R.N. Ravi in Tamil Nadu reaches an abrupt conclusion as the Home Ministry orchestrates a gubernatorial reshuffle ahead of crucial elections.

Nadda, and Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju slam Opposition for their behaviour inside Parliament

title: Passenger smokes ‘beedi’ on Delhi-Goa Akasa Air flight; case registered
content: As per the airline's complaint, the passenger allegedly smoked a 'beedi' (hand-rolled cigarette) inside the plane lavatory during the flight and was also found in possession of a lighter, which could pose a risk to the safety of the aircraft and passengers on board

title: How Dalit voting patterns have changed across elections
content: Post the 2014 Lok Sabha elections, Dalit voters have increasingly been consolidated by the BJP, away from the Congress

title: International Women’s Day: Shakuntala Grants launched to back women researchers and STEM education
content: Pixxel will provide micro-grants for women researchers working on projects related to climate resilience and environment

content: The custodial death of a tribal leader lodged in a jail in Chhattisgarh’s Kanker district has sparked a debate over prisoner safety across the State. A report on what transpired in the Assembly and the family’s claims

title: Only 10% of MPs/MLAs women, says ADR report
content: 74 (14%) are women among the 543 MPs in the 18th Lok Sabha . as many as 22 states and UTs have women MPs . only large state without a woman MP is Kerala .

content: For India, the Gulf is not a theatre. It is an artery. When an artery is under stress, what matters in the end is not the rhetoric of the response, but the steadiness of the hand.

content: Parliament Budget Session 2026 Today Live Updates: External Affairs S Jaishankar delivered a statement in the Parliament regarding the situation in the Middle East amid the escalating Iran war.

content: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) military helicopters have detected and shot down multiple Iranian drones before they could reach their target.

title: "Every Effort Being Made To Bring Back Stuck Indians": S Jaishankar On Iran War
content: Dialogue and diplomacy are the only way out of the Middle East crisis, and the government is closely monitoring developments in the region that is home to millions of Indians, External Affairs Minister Dr S Jaishankar said in Parliament today.

content: Observers say it's high time the former mayor evolved and grew, as well as became more mature, diplomatic and friendly with journalists, opponents and, most importantly, friendly neighbours like India and China.

title: "Right Thing To Do": S Jaishankar In Parliament On Iran Ship's Kochi Docking
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar told Parliament today that India had granted docking permission to an Iranian naval vessel at Kochi port on humanitarian grounds, describing the decision as "the right thing to do".

content: President Donald Trump promised that 2026 would be a bumper year for economic growth, but instead it has kicked off with job losses, rising gasoline prices and more uncertainty about America's future.

content: Mojtaba Khamenei has been named Iran's next supreme leader after his father, Ali Khamenei, was killed in the US-Israel strikes.

content: Stock Market News Today Live Updates: The Nifty50 and the Sensex fell sharply as a surge in crude oil prices.

content: Over a week into the US and Israel's unilateral declaration of war on Iran and the assassination of its Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Khamenei, reports have emerged linking Tehran to alleged plots to kill President Donald Trump.

title: Bangladesh Shuts Colleges Early For Eid To Save Power Amid Iran War Energy Crisis
content: Bangladesh will close all universities from Monday, bringing forward the Eid al-Fitr holidays as part of emergency measures to conserve electricity and fuel amid a worsening energy crisis linked to the conflict in the Middle East.

content: Vallabh Das Maheshwari had more than 32 years of experience in corporate governance and business management.

title: Asia's Ultra-Rich Having Second Thoughts On Dubai As Iran War Rages
content: Dubai's ascent as a burgeoning finance and wealth hub faces a test as the US-Israeli war with Iran hits close to home.

title: Iran's Big Gamble With Khamenei's Son Majotaba's Elevation As Supreme Leader
content: Iran's clerical leadership chose confrontation over compromise in appointing Mojtaba Khamenei to succeed his father, Ali Khamenei, a move regional officials say is a direct rebuke to US President Donald Trump, who had declared the son "unacceptable".

title: Has A Lok Sabha Speaker Been Impeached Before? What History Shows
content: The no-confidence notice against Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla was submitted by Opposition MPs in February and has the support of more than 100 members.

title: NCAA women's hockey tournament: Breaking down this year's field
content: the field is set for the 2026 NCAA women's hockey tournament . 11 teams looking to advance to the tournament on march 20 and 22 . the next six highest teams in the NPI rankings get at-large spots . Ohio State earned the top overall seed after beating Wisconsin 2-1 .

title: Men's Bracketology: Champ Week chaos is alive and well
content: Content extraction failed.

title: Ryan Blaney ends Tyler Reddick's streak, seals Penske sweep
content: Ryan Blaney wins team Penske's Cup Series race at Phoenix Raceway . the victory denies Tyler Reddick a fourth consecutive win to start the season . he chases down the 49th car he passed to seal his 18th career victory .

title: Tracking NFL free agency: Updates on re-signings, trades, cuts and rumors
content: free agency officially begins at 4 p.m. ET on monday, with the start of the new league year . teams can start negotiating and agreeing to terms with players at noon ET on Monday . the franchise/transition tag deadline passed with four players receiving one .

title: What does White House fight card say about the state of the UFC?
content: a six-fight slate for Freedom Fights 250 is set for June 14 in washington, d.c., on the white house South Lawn . defending champion Ilia Topuria and interim heavyweight champion Justin Gaethje will meet for the right to be called "undisputed"

title: Cafes, salons, community spaces bustle as Hyderabad celebrates Women’s Day
content: From small surprises at home to leisurely outings and self care breaks, residents found their own ways to honour the women in their lives and acknowledge the many roles they play in everyday life

title: One Nation, One Election — remedy worse than disease
content: India’s ‘One Nation, One Election’ proposal gravely undermines federalism, governance, democratic principles and the voter mandate

title: Tamil Nadu forms panel to frame rules to ban hostile dog breeds
content: The committee will recommend norms to regulate or ban aggressive dog breeds in the interest of public safety

title: DMK will meet its downfall for inducting ‘traitors’ ousted from AIADMK: Palaniswami
content: AIADMK recognises the individuals who work for it, unlike the DMK that prioritises the CM’s family; Magalir Urimai Thittam was implemented only after pressure from AIADMK, he says

title: Indian Women's Press Corps reiterates commitment to support safe, equitable spaces in media
content: It further said it was important that people listen to what women have to say in this complex, ever-changing world, adding that they must have a say in agendas that shape the policies, systems and indeed the world

title: Darjeeling district officials had raised alarm over lack of arrangements at venue
content: In the absence of the Chief Minister and Governor, the Minister-in-Waiting should have received the President according to protocol, officials said

title: On Women’s Day, cash incentives announced in Assam, Manipur
content: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says the government will transfer ₹9,000 each to around 40 lakh beneficiary families under the Orunodoi scheme

title: Rules not ready for new rural jobs Act
content: The government has to come up with “objective parameters” for normative allocation of budget to States, and formulate “development parameters” to categorise Gram Panchayats into three types as mandated

title: Government launches logo design contest for VB-G RAM G Act
content: The Ministry said it seeks to promote public participation in branding the programme, which is a key pillar of the government’s rural development strategy aligned with the vision of “Viksit Bharat 2047”

title: Jaishankar to speak on West Asia conflict in Parliament on March 9
content: Opposition has criticised government for its “silence” on Khamenei’s killing, torpedoing of Iranian ship by a U.S. submarine in the Indian ocean; India has “strongly condemned” the attacks on Gulf countries by Iran

title: Mamata should resign over protocol breach at President Murmu’s Darjeeling event: BJP
content: PM Modi had insulted the President by remaining seated with veteran BJP leader L.K. Advani while President Murmu stood next to them, says the West Bengal CM, holding a dharna against the SIR in Kolkata

title: ISRO and ESA sign agreement for Earth Observation missions
content: The agreement was signed on March 4 by M. Ganesh Pillai, scientific secretary, ISRO, and Simonetta Cheli, director, Earth Observation Programme, ESA, in a virtual meeting mode

title: Indian Railways launch ‘SHINE’ to empower women at workplace
content: Operational on International Women’s Day, the app enables women to lodge complaints of sexual harassment

title: NCB busts pan-India darknet drug network called ‘Team Kalki’
content: The two accused, who have been arrested, are ‘habitual offenders and were previously arrested in cases registered under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act”, the agency says

title: Children lead No-Plastic run on Krishna river bank
content: Over 500 participants join awareness run organised by AWARA and Aravinda Schools to highlight dangers of plastic pollution in Vijayawada

title: Uttam pledges to make Palamuru-Rangareddy fully operational in 30 months
content: Irrigation Minister vows to complete the Kalwakurthy, Nettempadu, Bhima and Koilsagar projects by March 2027 while reviewing the status of works on the projects; states that land acquisition has been given top priority

title: Rumours of export disruption trigger panic selling by aqua farmers in Andhra Pradesh
content: Farmers rush to harvest shrimp and sell at lower prices amid fears of shipping delays; officials say Israel-Iran conflict has little impact on marine exports from the State

title: India taps alternative crude supplies as Iran conflict drags on
content: Indian refiners have begun negotiating for additional crude cargoes from the U.S., Russia and West Africa to ensure supplies remain adequate; refineries, which convert crude oil into fuels like petrol and diesel, have deferred planned maintenance shutdowns and are maintaining normal processing rates to create buffers

title: From tariff turbulence to trade resilience, Andhra Pradesh anchors India’s shrimp export growth
content: After a 15% dip in U.S. shipments, Andhra Pradesh shifted 57% of exports to alternative markets, posting over US$2.5 billion in seafood exports in 2024-25

title: Tens of thousands of people attend 204th Sharanabasaveshwar Rathotsava
content: The historic rathotsava commemorates the death anniversary of 18th century saint and social reformer Sharanabasaveshwar and it continues to attract devotees cutting across caste, creed and communal lines

title: Kozhikode City police launches ‘Nirbhaya Nisha’
content: The initiative seeks to provide prompt police assistance and strengthen security arrangements for women commuting during later hours

title: Quantum leap for tribal girl students in NPTEL exam
content: 57 students, mostly girls from a remote Government Degree College in Chintapalli, appear for the quantum computing examination in Visakhapatnam after the Principal pays the ₹500 fee for those who could not afford it on International Women’s Day

title: Karnataka notifies reservation for 369 wards of new Bengaluru corporations, questions persist over quotas
content: However, the final notification is likely to be challenged in the court, as the Urban Development Department has not changed the percentage in either vertical reservation or the women quota, despite the draft notification drawing widespread criticism over the same.

title: UConn's Strong, Fudd headline women's Wooden Award top 15
content: analysts believe the key matchups will be key to the SEC Tournament title tilt between the Gamecocks and Longhorns (2:09) Azzi Fudd and Sarah Strong of the undefeated UConn Huskies headline the John R. Wooden Award top 15 ballot . the award is given annually to the best college basketball player in the country .

title: Watch: Video challenges Trump’s claim, shows US Tomahawk missile near Iran school
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers.
